Q1: The types of event map but only up to certain point. For example flooding and fires are common for both areas and I'd expect the benchmark to help me to see how well models can capture general damage type and in description. If translated, I could also see how this performance is lost. But the reality of the region can be quite different. I'd expect responders to focus more on the international borders and strategic economic routes since those are the ones who would mainly reported in the news. Bu with regards of social media, I do believe this benchmark would be quite limited to represent how well a model understands about the regional socioeconomic context.
Q2: Partially yes. I would expect the system to be used to analyze content in regional Spanish, mainly Rio Platense Spanish, that contains regional vocabulary (e.g. location and names in those indigenous languages for example).
Q3:
A flooded dirt road would need to be severe enough to appear in the news or social media, affecting semi-urban areas and more rural areas as well. I don't expect only a road affecting an indigenous community area would be the case. I think the label for this scenario would depend on how the consequences are framed. For example, humans lost, inhabitants without water neither food supplies for an extended period of time, all routes in the area affected preventing commercial trucks' mobility.
National coordinators would be the ones assigned to label
Q4: The system would need to produce simultaneous labels of impact types at the minimum. I wouldn't expect the system to be good enough to rank the local data accurate enough to relied directly on the system output without human intervention/check. The system output format would serve differently for those users. For the gendarme, a fast iteration could facilitate to find information for particular event happening in an ongoing disaster (e.g. in a flood, a gas truck, a school bus stuck in summer with high temperatures). For a national coordinator in Buenos Aires, the system output would be useful at scale for decision-making, for example to asses the national needs and allocate limited national and public resources (e.g. water bombing helicopter, equipped and trained emergency rescue).
